expectations.evidence: profiles array contains 4 entries: CIO/VP IT, CISO/Head IT Security, Head of Procurement/Einkauf, Head of Cloud Platform/Enterprise Architect.

expectations.text: Each profile has role, seniority, pain_points (max 5), buying_criteria (max 5), information_sources, decision_role
expectations.evidence: All 4 profiles have role, seniority, pain_points (4-5 items each, all ≤5), buying_criteria (5 items each), information_sources (5 each), decision_role. All fields present and within limits.

expectations.text: buying_committee object present with typical_size, decision_model, deal_stall_points
expectations.evidence: buying_committee object present with size ('5-8 stakeholders'), decision_model ('Consensus-driven with CIO as final economic buyer...'), typical_deal_cycle, deal_size, and stall_points (6 items with specific timelines).

expectations.text: 3 named customers present (E.ON, RWE, EnBW) with fit_score and source_urls
expectations.evidence: named_customers array has 3 entries: E.ON SE (fit_score: high, 2 source_urls), RWE AG (fit_score: high, 3 source_urls), EnBW (fit_score: high, 3 source_urls).
assessment-round1.json and assessment.json both follow the customer-review-assessor schema exactly: 3 perspectives, 5 criteria each, weights, synthesis, verdict.

expectations.text: All three perspective scores >= 70 in final assessment
expectations.evidence: Final scores: reviewer=97, cso=94, market_expert=97. All well above 70.

expectations.text: Content language matches portfolio.json language field (de)
expectations.evidence: Pain points, buying criteria, and named customer content are in German: 'SAP IS-U end-of-life (ECC support ends 2027) forces a cloud migration decision...' However, profile descriptions mix German domain terms with English sentence structure. Field names correctly remain English per skill spec.

expectations.text: At least one veto-holder role present (CISO, Compliance, or Einkauf)
expectations.evidence: Two veto-holders: CISO ('Veto-holder — must approve all IT service providers from a security and compliance perspective') and Einkauf ('Has veto power over non-compliant suppliers').

expectations.text: Pain points reference specific regulatory deadlines (NIS2, KRITIS, SAP IS-U)
expectations.evidence: CIO: 'SAP IS-U end-of-life (ECC support ends 2027)', 'NIS2 transposition into German law (effective October 2026)'. CISO: 'NIS2 Article 21', 'BSI KRITIS audits (§8a BSIG)'. Einkauf: 'SektVO... EUR 431K threshold'.

expectations.text: Named customers have fit_rationale explaining score
expectations.evidence: All 3 named customers have detailed fit_rationale: E.ON ('Existing T-Systems reference for SAP workload migration...'), RWE ('actively executing a major SAP cloud transformation via RISE with SAP...'), EnBW ('Recently completed a major IT transformation replacing SAP IS-U with powercloud...').

suggestions.assertion: Content language matches portfolio.json language field (de)
suggestions.reason: Pain points mix German terms with English sentence structure ('SAP IS-U end-of-life forces a cloud migration decision'). The skill spec says content should be in portfolio language (de). A stricter assertion would check that pain_points and buying_criteria are predominantly German, not English with embedded Fachbegriffe.

Revision loop was not exercised (accept on round 1). The assertion C-05 (assessor invoked at least once) passes trivially. A stronger test would use deliberately weak input or a stricter assessor to trigger the revise path.

eval_feedback.overall: All 10 assertions pass. Output quality is high — well-differentiated profiles, canonical schema, detailed named customer research. The language observation (English structure with German terms) is the main area for skill improvement.
